# Enchantment Forge

A **data-driven enchanting/forging** mod inspired by **Stardew Valley‚Äôs Forge**. Create custom recipes with datapacks (no code), tune the crafting speed, and use a simple command to adjust player XP.

- **Data-driven**: all recipes come from datapacks  
- **Configurable**: tweak **crafting speed** via config  
- **Stardew-style workflow**: combine tools, fuel, and gems  
- **One admin command** to manage XP quickly

---

## üîß How it Works (Quick)
- Drop a datapack with your recipes ‚Üí the mod reads them at load time.  
- Use the **Datapack Generator** to avoid hand-editing JSON:  
  - **Generator:** https://spacemex.github.io/EnchantmentForgeing-Site/
- (Optional) Adjust **crafting speed** in the mod‚Äôs config.

---

## ‚å®Ô∏è Command
`/take level|points player amount`
- `level` ‚Üí removes experience **levels**  
- `points` ‚Üí removes raw **XP points**

**Example**

---

## üß© Placeholders for Commands
You can use placeholders inside **datapack-defined commands**. They are replaced at runtime:

| Placeholder        | Resolves to                                  |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| `%player%`         | The target player‚Äôs name/selector             |
| `%player_coords%`  | The player‚Äôs current coordinates in the world |

**Examples**
```text
/take level %player% 5
/summon minecraft:lighting_bolt %player_coords%
```

---

## ‚öôÔ∏è Configuration
- **Crafting Speed**: configurable in the mod‚Äôs config file (lower = faster, higher = slower).  
- Safe for servers and modpacks.

---

## üß© Datapacks
- Fully **data-driven** 
- Use the **Datapack Generator** (recommended):

## ‚ùì FAQ

**Q: Will this be updated to version X?**  
**A:** At some point‚Äîonce I wrap my head around Mojang‚Äôs new methods and API changes.

**Q: How do I make a recipe / get it working?**  
**A:** Use the Datapack Generator ‚Üí https://spacemex.github.io/EnchantmentForgeing-Site/  
Download the datapack, place it in your `<world>/datapacks` folder, then run `/reload` or restart the game/server. (Both a zipped `.zip` or an unpacked folder work.)

**Q: Will this support Launcher X?**  
**A:** Same as Q1‚Äîsupport follows once the mod is updated for that Minecraft version.

**Q: Can I use this in my modpack?**  
**A:** Yes‚Äîno attribution required.
